summary: Install-time DNS exfiltration of hostname and username to Burp Collaborator
details: At npm install time, the package's preinstall lifecycle script (preinstall.js) reads the installer's OS hostname (os.hostname()) and username (os.userInfo().username), concatenates them into a DNS label, and performs a dns.lookup against a subdomain of oastify.com (Burp Collaborator out-of-band callback infrastructure controlled by the package author). This causes any machine that runs `npm install` on this package — including developer workstations and CI runners — to leak identifying information (internal hostname and user account) over DNS to a third party. The package self-describes as a bug-bounty dependency-confusion research placeholder targeting a specific organization, but the probe fires unconditionally for every installer, not just for the intended target, and collects data without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
